eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
fen: noun. A type of wetland fed by ground water and runoff, containing peat below the waterline, characteristically alkaline.
fon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Fon.
font: noun. A receptacle in a church for holy water, especially one used in baptism.
foot: noun. A biological structure found in many animals that is used for locomotion and that is frequently a separate organ at the terminal part
footnote: noun. A short piece of text, often numbered, placed at the bottom of a printed page, that adds a comment, citation, reference etc, to a
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nett: adjective. Alternative spelling of net (remaining after expenses or deductions).
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.
often: adverb. Frequently; many times on different occasions.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
onto: preposition. Arriving upon or on top of (speaking of a physical or metaphorical movement).
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tent: noun. A pavilion or portable lodge consisting of skins, canvas, or some strong cloth, stretched and sustained by poles, used for sheltering
tet: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tetum.
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
too: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xicotepec de Juárez Totonac.
toon: noun. A cartoon, especially an animated television show.
toot: noun. The noise of a horn or whistle.
tot: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Patla-Chicontla Totonac.
foo: noun. Alternative form of fu: an administrative subdivision of imperial China; the capital of such divisions.
nef: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nefamese.
ofo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ofo.
oof: interjection. A sound mimicking the loss of air, as if someone's solar plexus had just been struck.
oon: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Önge.
oto: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Oto-Pamean languages.
tef: noun. Used as a specific epithet to indicate a kind of grass
tnt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tontemboan.
tonto: noun. Synonym of idiot: someone who is foolish or stupid.
toto: noun. Only used in in toto
